title: Kaji and Misato pure for each other
display_title: Kaji and Misato pure for each other
wikitext:
[]: Kaji & Misato: "pure" for each other?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 02, 2013 11:41 am [Split from "So what did Asuka see in Kaji?" . The Guy Nacks quote below represents the off-topic part of a post the rest of which was left in the original topic. EDIT: The portion which was on-topic in the original topic but off-topic here has been included because Kendrix might have been responding to both parts. - Monk] Guy Nacks wrote: There's no evidence whatsoever that Asuka's feelings are reciprocated by Kaji. In fact, you can even argue quite successfully that he was trying to set her up with Shinji. Kaji's got this reputation as a player the same way Misato has a reputation as a loose party-girl. The truth about Misato is though is that she's only ever slept with one person. Is it that far of a stretch to assume Misato might be the only one Kaji's been with or has truly desired?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 02, 2013 11:45 am Kendrix wrote: his last message pretty much implied he wanted to marry her

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 02, 2013 11:46 am Kaji and Misato being "faithful" to each other doesn't have anything to do with purity. They're both too screwed up to move on from an obviously toxic relationship. If you get into an intense teenage/college fling you either marry the person or move on; getting hung up on them for life is a Bad Thing. His blatant attempts to push Asuka towards Shinji aren't necessarily healthy, either. [Responses to this line, which was on-topic in its original context, go in this topic. - Monk] E: I always assumed the words he couldn't say were "I love you", not a marriage proposal. Never even thought of that.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 02, 2013 11:56 am Chuckman wrote: I always assumed the words he couldn't say were "I love you", not a marriage proposal. Never even thought of that.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue Jul 02, 2013 12:22 pm I think Kaji possibly realises the flaws in his relationship with Misato, but still wants to pursue a committed relationship with her. Hence his final message. Misato also possibly was hoping to enter such a relationship with Kaji or was deeply moved by his wish to enter a relationship with her, thus she breaks down in tears after she listens to Kaji's final message and realises he won't be coming back and they'll never be able to pursue their relationship.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jul 02, 2013 1:09 pm Misato and Kaji are the most obvious "true love" couple in all of Evangelion. Consequently, they are probably the least shipped couple in the entire fandom. Go figure. But this idea of a "pure" or exclusive relationship between isn't the most solid of conclusions. True, we are never shown evidence of anyone having any relationships other than those shown onscreen, but that doesn't mean that everyone in the show has to be single. The underlying assumption in fiction is that characters do exist offscreen in some fashion. Is it valid to assume, say, Aoba is single throughout the series? Perhaps it's more valid to assume he had groupies. His transition guide Rei's could be taken either way. But either assumption is largely unimportant. The biggest evidence for Misato being single is here line to Kaji in #20 Episode #20 wrote: Kaji : I didn't know you still smoked. Misato: I only smoke after this sort of thing. So nobody knows but you. Kaji: Well, I'm honored.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 02, 2013 3:07 pm The ambiguity in the smoking line is a false ambiguity- it's clear she's had no other sexual partners. She's afraid of sex and thinks it's dirty, and consequently that she must be trashy because she she enjoys it, so she makes herself trashy to fit her mental image of a sexual being/punish herself for experiencing pleasure during the act. Her trashy drunken whore persona in turn armors her from human contact with anyone else, but doesn't work on him because he knows her better, knows it's a defense mechanism, and cares about her enough to push through it. Whether Anno has experience of it himself or not, this is something that real women in the real world have been known to do. Evangelion as a feminist work highlights the pain caused by living up to the pressure of the madonna/whore complex.

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Tue Jul 02, 2013 3:31 pm I always assumed the words he couldn't say were "I love you", not a marriage proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 02, 2013 3:43 pm She knew she wasn't delivering on that promise but made it anyway to show him she really cared, because she's an incredibly fucked up person and can't separate sexual/romantic affections from familial ones as a result of her trauma. I stated my reasons why Kaji would never touch Asuka in the thread that this one was split off from. I'm equally firm in my convictions that if Shinji had been receptive to her advances at any point, she would have had sex with him. Regretted it later, maybe even gone on a bender or become suicidal, but she would have done it. Once he's inside her armor there's no distinction between "adopted little brother" and "potential lover" to her. It isn't manipulation- to her, Shinji being willing to couple with her despite her drunken slobbery and her scar would be absolute proof that he's accepted her, and she's desperate for acceptance. Her sexual advances towards him ramp up from playful and flirtatious to serious after Kaji dies; he's the only one left, in a nice little parallel to the way Shinji in turn treats Asuka. I think you're off on Kaji's character, there. He presents a certain image, but that image is armor the way Misato's is. For him, it's practical as well as psychological. By creeping on Ritsuko and Maya he affronts them, Maya in particular, and makes sure that neither of them will pay too much attention or probe too deeply into his activities due to their distaste for him. In times of stress or when he has no need for his facade, he's fatherly, introspective, and philosophical. It's probably those qualities that wooed Misato in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Tue Jul 02, 2013 4:51 pm I think you're off on Kaji's character, there. He presents a certain image, but that image is armor the way Misato's is. For him, it's practical as well as psychological. By creeping on Ritsuko and Maya he affronts them, Maya in particular, and makes sure that neither of them will pay too much attention or probe too deeply into his activities due to their distaste for him.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jul 02, 2013 4:59 pm Chuckman wrote: Whether Anno has experience of it himself or not, this is something that real women in the real world have been known to do. Evangelion as a feminist work highlights the pain caused by living up to the pressure of the madonna/whore complex.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 02, 2013 5:33 pm Really, I don't get how the Misato/Kaji relationship somehow didn't work or was "toxic"§ to them; He obviously helps to *relieve* them complexes in ep 15. From what we learn, they were also very happy; Misato's complexes are more her own little package which then got in the way as an adult; In some way, they both feel they don't deserve the happy, positive relationship they had BC survivor guilt, so Misato saw dumping him as a way to punish herself, aside from the whole father complex, and he didn't fight more to keep her for similar reasons (He just bought the "There's another guy" explanation.) Episode 25 also has Misato say that Kaji "took her innocence". Saying they were hung op on each other isn't really that true either; They just happened to be stuffed into a situation where they were at the same workplace. Misato was mainly single because she was busy preparing for the angel-killing/with her job/ no Japanese man wants a lady with a paycheck. (deliberate exaggeration) Point in case, if she really was that way, why not get boned by the perfectly willing, young and firm Hyuuga? Never noticed the cigarette quote in that way, but it also adds to that theory. Also note how the "marriage" thing was kinda a recurring thing with Misato, see a comparable dialogue in ep 15. The whole "slut" monologue is internalized cathegorism that women who don't fit into that "proper lady" BS must face today; and a bit of residual "You have to be a good girl.", plus massive self-doubts/hatred that needn't reflect the truth. It's a real life phenomenon. In many ways, EVA set out to completely thrash this purity complex so prevalent in other anime. See also Shinji and Asuka, or the ugly truth of what teenage flings that are often idealized to "pure" relationships are really like, big keywords being HORMONES, LUST and EGO. This kinda resulted in the people who simply will not hear that if you shouted it into their ears with a megaphone taking Rei as their mascot because no one else fit/she's the one person Shinji manages to have meaningful, deep communications with. But I can see Anno headdesking about that because Rei is supposed to be what you can earn if you dare reach out, the hope that people can understand each other, the reason to stay. Unlike most anime-protagonists, walking on the girl naked did NOT magically earn her. It didn't help, and the scene illustrates quite well why it shouldn't . To earn her respect, he had to stop thinking about his own fears/problems and go talk to her/face a scary monster that nearly killed him. So having ppl confusing Rei with gratis fuck is as if you try to motivate ppl to do something by offering them a cake, and they take the cake and don't do the work... ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Misato and Kaji are the most obvious "true love" couple in all of Evangelion. Consequently, they are probably the least shipped couple in the entire fandom. Go figure.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 02, 2013 6:23 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The problem here is that it's difficult for someone to keep up a "trashy drunken whore persona" without ever actually having sex with anyone for a decade or so. The next best conclusion is that Misato is in fact, simply a lonely drunk, which while in keeping with the general tone of NGE, does not fit her words in #25 very well. Actually, I'm no longer sure Misato even gives off such a personality in any serious way (Notwithstanding the fact that she is a functioning alcoholic). Misato's exclusiveness to Kaji -- Shinji excepted-- might be a lot easier to understand if we're not reading unjustified stereotypes into the analysis. You could also assume that Misato act is only a recent one, but again this goes back to making assumptions about offscreen behaviour which have no bearing on the plot. We could just as easily assume that Misato lived life as a lesbian before arriving at Tokyo-3 for example. It wouldn't contradict any other the arguments made in this entire thread, but it wouldn't explain anything either.

[]:             Quote Post by AlphaGamma » Tue Jul 02, 2013 8:26 pm Chuckman wrote: Her sexual advances towards him ramp up from playful and flirtatious to serious after Kaji dies; he's the only one left, in a nice little parallel to the way Shinji in turn treats Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jul 02, 2013 8:29 pm Get enough beer in her... No. She'd snapped him out of it before without offering him a roll in the hay. She did that for a reason and that reason is tied to her knowledge that it was her last moments. Misato loves Shinji -look at how she behaves after he's absorbed into the Eva, and when he returns- but she's not in love with him; she has a hard time distinguishing what that means and can't extricate sex from platonic/familial love.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 02, 2013 8:37 pm Chuckman wrote: she has a hard time distinguishing what that means and can't extricate sex from platonic/familial love.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 03, 2013 4:56 am Kendrix wrote: Also, someone please do away with the stereotypical title or at least but ""s around the "pure" before it gives someone eyecancer.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 03, 2013 7:12 pm Chuckman wrote: Does she even project the drunken whore persona outside of her home?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 03, 2013 7:32 pm Rebuild Misato's alcoholism discussion split out to this rebuild thread ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Rebuild offers a slightly different take on this, with Misato actually appearing drunk on duty in 2.0, and generally a heavier drinker in general. NGE's Misato on the other hand appears to be more restrained when away from her apartment.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 04, 2013 11:20 am Kendrix wrote: ...because these things are more about society's perception of sex lives, (snip) This doesn't mean she is; It's a realistic depiction of how such things can screw you up/make you doubt everything yourself.
